prompt:
Our session-token cache goes insane every time the fleet's NTP daemon steps the clock. Saturday it stepped backward ~1h and tokens that should have aged out kept getting served for the rest of the hour — auth saw revoked sessions come back from the dead. When the clock stepped forward, the opposite: freshly cached tokens all vanished at once and the origin got hammered. We also have a slow-burn report that touch()-refreshed tokens still die at their original deadline, like the refresh only cosmetically updates the 'last seen' display time.

The cache takes an injected clock with BOTH monotonic() and wall() on it, so this should be fixable without new machinery. Contract, as I understand it: entry age and expiry are immune to wall corrections; an entry is expired the instant its age reaches the ttl (not one tick later); inspect() keeps showing real wall timestamps for the dashboard; eviction kicks out the genuinely oldest live entry even if the wall clock lurched around between puts; stats stay exact.

`python3 test_ttlcache.py` reproduces all of it with a scripted clock (it drives normal ticks and one-sided wall jumps separately). Fix ttlcache.py — don't touch the tests, don't change the public API.
